story: GVEA (Golden Valley Electric Association) weighs a second ~45MW GE LM6000 naphtha turbine for its North Pole Power Plant; board vote scheduled July 28, 2026

sources.title: GVEA board to weigh heavy purchase decision in July, as members raise concerns
sources.note: Board vote date, ~$120M/45-58MW estimate, Enstar contract end, 250,000 gal/day diesel figure, one of the two Patrice Lee quotes

sources.title: North Pole turbine proposal sparks clean-air concerns
sources.note: $50,000 order-slot reservation (inside a larger April board approval), ~$90M/45MW estimate, PM2.5 non-attainment zone confirmation, 200,000 gal/day diesel figure, 36MW Ameresco Delta Wind + battery storage detail, other Patrice Lee quote

sources.title: CEO's Message: July 2026
sources.note: GVEA's own framing of the upcoming board decision on a second LM6000 or alternative

sources.title: Data centers drive record surge in GE Vernova power equipment orders as turbine slots tighten through 2030
sources.note: GE Vernova ~100GW gas-turbine backlog, ~20% tied to data centers

sources.title: Siemens Energy's gas turbine boom drives highest-ever order backlog
sources.note: About 60% (not an exact 60-65% range) of Siemens Energy's 14GW of gas-turbine orders (2025 year-to-date as of Aug 2025) were for data centers

notes: The Patrice Lee 'You cannot add to the pollution... be legally correct' line and the 'dangerous situation' line are two separate genuine quotes from two different News-Miner articles; treat as attributed to her across both pieces, not one continuous quote.
Cost is shown as a range ($90M-$120M) because the two News-Miner articles give different figures.
AI/data-center attribution is this dispatch's analysis, grounded in the GE Vernova/Siemens backlog reporting above, not GVEA's own stated reason for the purchase.
Board vote had NOT happened as of 2026-07-19 (scheduled July 28, 2026) - framed as pending, not a done deal.
